facebook.content_angle: Post-NZ Age Group Champs squad recognition — the collective effort, club identity, and team culture that defines competitive swimming, timely after Nationals just wrapped at Hawke's Bay
facebook.image_prompt: swim club warming up in dark cinematic indoor pool, coach briefing athletes at lane edge pre-race, dramatic orange-amber overhead spotlights, deep teal water, multiple swimmers in sleek dark racing suits no logos, high contrast chiaroscuro lighting, professional sports photography, bottom area dark for text overlay, photorealistic 8K
facebook.overlay_text:
Your squad earned this.
Jaked performance swimwear.
velocx.co.nz
facebook.caption:
Five days. Ten sessions. Every swimmer in that lane trained for this moment.

The 2026 NZ Age Group Championships just wrapped at the Hawke's Bay Regional Aquatic Centre — and somewhere in those heats, a club squad showed up and gave everything they had.

That's the thing about competitive swimming that outsiders never quite understand.

👉 The hours at 5am aren't just for the individual — they're for the lane you share
👉 Every PB carries the weight of every set your squad pushed you through
👉 When one swimmer drops time, the whole lane feels it

You don't become a competitor alone. You become one in a squad.

Jaked race suits are trusted by athletes who understand that performance is built in the company of others — engineered in Italy, worn by swimmers who show up for themselves and for their squad.

Were you or your club at NZ Age Groups this year?
facebook.engagement_bait: Tag your squad below — and drop how many PBs your club picked up at Nationals! 🏊
